Paper Title: U-Net v2: Rethinking the Skip Connections of U-Net for Medical Image Segmentation
Core Method: Hadamard product-based skip connections infusing multi-level semantic info
Key Contribution: Enriches skip features via cross-level semantic & detail infusion; plug-and-play
Limitations / Gap: No frequency decomposition; no explicit boundary supervision
Relevance to Our Work: Direct competitor — our WBS module adds wavelet decomposition + boundary guidance on top of improved skip connections

Paper Title: UDTransNet: Narrowing Semantic Gaps in U-Net with Learnable Skip Connections
Core Method: Dual Attention Transformer (DAT) + Decoder-guided Recalibration in skip connections
Key Contribution: Learnable skip connections bridging semantic gaps via channel+spatial attention
Limitations / Gap: Transformer-based — heavier; no frequency domain analysis
Relevance to Our Work: Shows attention in skip connections works; we replace Transformer with lightweight wavelet+boundary attention

Paper Title: TA-MoSC: Task-Adaptive Mixture of Skip Connections
Core Method: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) routing for skip connections
Key Contribution: Task-adaptive routing selects expert combinations at each decoder stage
Limitations / Gap: No frequency awareness; complex routing mechanism
Relevance to Our Work: Different philosophy (routing vs. decomposition); complementary not competing

Paper Title: SK-VM++: Mamba Assists Skip-Connections
Core Method: Mamba-based VSS blocks in skip connections (UNet++ framework)
Key Contribution: Mamba refines high-low feature fusion in skip connections with linear complexity
Limitations / Gap: Mamba-dependent; not plug-and-play on arbitrary backbones
Relevance to Our Work: Uses Mamba in skips; we use lightweight wavelet — simpler, backbone-agnostic

Paper Title: R2U++: Multiscale Recurrent Residual U-Net with Dense Skip Connections
Core Method: Recurrent residual conv blocks + dense skip pathways
Key Contribution: Combines deeper feature extraction with dense multi-scale skip connections
Limitations / Gap: No attention mechanism; no frequency decomposition
Relevance to Our Work: Baseline approach showing dense skips help; our method adds wavelet intelligence

Paper Title: Rethinking U-net Skip Connections for Biomedical Image Segmentation
Core Method: Analysis of removing skip connections at different levels; domain shift study
Key Contribution: Best performance when removing uppermost skip connection (reduces domain-specific noise)
Limitations / Gap: Analytical study only; no constructive module proposed
Relevance to Our Work: Motivates our work: shallow skip connections carry noise — wavelet filtering addresses this

Paper Title: FreqFusion: Frequency-Aware Feature Fusion for Dense Image Prediction
Core Method: Adaptive Low-Pass Filter + High-Pass Filter generators for feature fusion
Key Contribution: Addresses intra-category inconsistency and boundary displacement via frequency-aware fusion
Limitations / Gap: General dense prediction; not designed for medical skip connections specifically
Relevance to Our Work: KEY INSPIRATION — proves frequency-aware fusion improves boundaries; we bring this into medical U-Net skips

Paper Title: WRANet: Wavelet Integrated Residual Attention U-Net
Core Method: DWT replaces downsampling; removes high-freq to reduce noise; attention in decoder
Key Contribution: Wavelet downsampling reduces noise interference; attention recovers lost features
Limitations / Gap: REMOVES high-freq as noise — opposite of preserving boundary info; wavelet not in skips
Relevance to Our Work: Contrasting approach: they discard high-freq, we selectively enhance it with boundary supervision

Paper Title: MFR-UNet: Medical Image Segmentation with Fused Multi-Scale Feature Refinement
Core Method: Wavelet transform convolution (WtConv) in encoder + LRFA + weighted context fusion
Key Contribution: Wavelet in encoder for freq-domain feature learning; large receptive field attention
Limitations / Gap: Wavelet only in encoder, NOT in skip connections; standard fusion in skips
Relevance to Our Work: Proves wavelet features help in encoder; our novelty is placing wavelet IN skip connections

Paper Title: SFFNet: Wavelet-Based Spatial and Frequency Domain Fusion
Core Method: Two-stage network: spatial features first, then wavelet decomposition + dual-cross attention
Key Contribution: WTFD decomposes features via Haar wavelet; MDAF bridges frequency-spatial semantic gap
Limitations / Gap: Remote sensing domain; two-stage pipeline not modular for medical U-Net
Relevance to Our Work: Shows wavelet + attention works for segmentation; we adapt for medical skip connections

Paper Title: WaveFormer: Unlocking Fine-Grained Details with Wavelet-based HF Enhancement
Core Method: Wavelet decomposition + Gaussian pyramid on HF + multi-scale context in skip connections
Key Contribution: Reformulates self-attention in frequency domain; multi-scale skip context enhancement
Limitations / Gap: Transformer-based (heavy); wavelet applied to self-attention, not directly to skip features
Relevance to Our Work: Related but different: they modify attention; we modify the skip features themselves before fusion

Paper Title: EW-ViT: Frequency-Domain Refinement of Vision Transformers
Core Method: Wavelet decomposition within self-attention + Prompt-Guided HF Refiner for degradation
Key Contribution: Adaptive frequency refinement in ViT; handles image degradation via HF prompts
Limitations / Gap: ViT-specific; not applicable to CNN-based U-Net; focuses on degradation not boundaries
Relevance to Our Work: Shows wavelet decomposition helps segmentation quality; different application context

Paper Title: Wavelet Feature Enhancement (WFE) for Remote Sensing
Core Method: Hierarchical wavelet decomposition fed back to corresponding network layers
Key Contribution: Multi-scale wavelet features enhance CNN/Transformer backbones with minimal compute
Limitations / Gap: Remote sensing only; feed-forward injection, not skip connection modification
Relevance to Our Work: Confirms wavelet enhancement is lightweight and effective; supports our design choice

Paper Title: WA-NET: Boundary-Aware Skin Lesion Segmentation via Frequency-Spatial Fusion
Core Method: Boundary Refinement Module (BRM) + Enhanced Wavelet Transform (EWT) + composite loss
Key Contribution: Separate boundary branch + wavelet module for texture; edge-supervised loss
Limitations / Gap: Wavelet and boundary are SEPARATE modules, not unified in skip connections
Relevance to Our Work: CLOSEST competitor — key difference: they have separate branches; we unify wavelet+boundary IN skip connections

Paper Title: EASNet: Edge-Aware Segmentation with Boundary-Aware and Frequency Attention
Core Method: DCT + DWT for multi-scale frequency info + boundary-driven criss-cross attention + hybrid loss
Key Contribution: Combines DCT and DWT for frequency features; BDCC attention for spatial dependencies
Limitations / Gap: Complex architecture; DCT+DWT together is heavy; not plug-and-play
Relevance to Our Work: CLOSE competitor — uses both DCT and DWT which is heavier; our approach is simpler and modular

Paper Title: SkinMamba: Cross-Scale Global State Modeling + Frequency Boundary Guidance
Core Method: Mamba-based encoder + Frequency Boundary Guided Module (FBGM) using wavelet
Key Contribution: SRSSB for global context; FBGM provides boundary priors via frequency decomposition
Limitations / Gap: Mamba-dependent architecture; FBGM is auxiliary, not in skip connections
Relevance to Our Work: Mamba+freq+boundary exists BUT tied to Mamba backbone; ours is backbone-agnostic

Paper Title: BCF-UNet: Boundary-guided Context Fusion U-Net
Core Method: Adaptive Multi-Frequency Encoder (AMFE) + Boundary-aware Pyramid Module (BAPM) + AKAFM fusion
Key Contribution: WT-inspired multi-freq encoder; boundary pyramid extraction; multi-scale fusion
Limitations / Gap: Boundary and frequency are separate modules in encoder, not unified in skip connections
Relevance to Our Work: Boundary + frequency explored but in encoder; our novelty is the skip connection location

Paper Title: BRNet: Boundary Refinement Network for Polyp Segmentation
Core Method: Boundary Generation Module (BGM) + boundary-guided refinement at each decoder layer
Key Contribution: Fuses low-level spatial + high-level concepts for boundary maps; top-down deep supervision
Limitations / Gap: No frequency domain analysis; boundary from spatial features only
Relevance to Our Work: Strong baseline; we add frequency-domain decomposition to improve boundary detection quality

Paper Title: BMANet: Boundary-guided Multi-level Attention for Polyp Segmentation
Core Method: Cascaded Partial Decoder + Boundary Aware Module + Boundary-guided Multi-level Attention
Key Contribution: BAM combines low-level and global features for boundary; BMA integrates multi-level features
Limitations / Gap: Purely spatial domain; no frequency-based boundary enhancement
Relevance to Our Work: Good baseline; demonstrates boundary attention helps polyps; we add wavelet for better edges

Paper Title: MEGANet: Multi-Scale Edge-Guided Attention Network
Core Method: Laplacian Operator-based Edge-Guided Attention (EGA) module in encoder-decoder
Key Contribution: Classical edge detection (Laplacian) + attention preserves HF info as network deepens
Limitations / Gap: Laplacian only (single frequency); no wavelet multi-scale decomposition
Relevance to Our Work: Uses Laplacian for edges — we use wavelet which provides multi-scale frequency decomposition

Paper Title: BUNet: Boundary Uncertainty Aware Network
Core Method: PVT encoder + Boundary Exploration Module + Boundary Uncertainty Module
Key Contribution: Explores error-prone boundary regions; coarse-to-fine polyp segmentation
Limitations / Gap: No frequency domain; uncertainty-based, not frequency-based boundary detection
Relevance to Our Work: Complementary approach (uncertainty vs. frequency); could combine with our method

Paper Title: BGDiffSeg: Wavelet Diffusion + Boundary Enhancement for Skin Lesion
Core Method: Wavelet-transform-based diffusion + Diffusion Boundary Enhancement Module + Bidirectional Attention
Key Contribution: Wavelet speeds up diffusion; DBEM enhances boundary in diffusion process
Limitations / Gap: Diffusion-based (very different paradigm); boundary enhancement tied to diffusion process
Relevance to Our Work: Different paradigm entirely (diffusion); wavelet+boundary used but in generation context

Paper Title: AdaptUNet: Wavelet Transformation + U-Net for Polyp Segmentation
Core Method: Customized U-Net + attention skip connections + wavelet feature extraction
Key Contribution: Wavelet extracts overlooked features; attention in skip connections for fusion
Limitations / Gap: Wavelet as separate preprocessing; attention in skips is basic; not unified
Relevance to Our Work: Closest to our work in spirit BUT wavelet is preprocessing, not decomposition within skips

Paper Title: HCViT-Net: Hybrid CNN-ViT with Wavelet-Guided Attention Refinement
Core Method: MobileNet encoder + MSQFormer + Wavelet-guided Attention Refinement Module (WARM) in skip
Key Contribution: WARM on highest-resolution skip connection; wavelet enhances boundary details in skip
Limitations / Gap: WARM only on ONE skip level (highest res); no boundary supervision loss
Relevance to Our Work: VERY RELEVANT — wavelet in skip connection exists BUT only at one level and without boundary GT supervision

Paper Title: VM-UNet: Vision Mamba UNet for Medical Image Segmentation
Core Method: Pure SSM-based U-shape architecture using Visual State Space (VSS) blocks
Key Contribution: First pure SSM-based medical segmentation model; linear complexity
Limitations / Gap: Pure Mamba; standard skip connections; no boundary or frequency awareness
Relevance to Our Work: Important baseline to compare against; our WBS module could plug into VM-UNet too

Paper Title: U-Mamba: Enhancing Long-range Dependency for Biomedical Image Segmentation
Core Method: Hybrid CNN-SSM block in U-Net; self-configuring mechanism
Key Contribution: Combines CNN local features + SSM long-range; auto-adapts to datasets
Limitations / Gap: Standard skip connections; no frequency or boundary enhancement
Relevance to Our Work: Key baseline; demonstrates hybrid CNN-SSM value; our module is complementary

Paper Title: PMFSNet: Polarized Multi-scale Feature Self-attention Network (Lightweight)
Core Method: Lightweight UNet + PMFS block (attention-based multi-scale feature enhancement)
Key Contribution: Sub-1M parameter model with competitive performance; plug-and-play PMFS block
Limitations / Gap: No frequency domain; lightweight but purely spatial attention
Relevance to Our Work: Lightweight baseline; demonstrates small models can compete; we target similar efficiency

Paper Title: INA-Net: Integrated Noise-Adaptive Attention for Skin Lesion Segmentation
Core Method: Edge-aware Spatial Attention + Multi-scale Channel Attention + Fourier Wavelet Analysis
Key Contribution: FW-Parser handles high-freq noise; ESA enhances boundary perception
Limitations / Gap: Fourier+Wavelet for noise handling, not for skip connection feature decomposition
Relevance to Our Work: Related frequency approach but for noise robustness; different goal than our boundary enhancement

Paper Title: Frequency-domain Dual-domain Consistency for Semi-supervised Skin Lesion Seg.
Core Method: DWT-based frequency perturbation + dual-branch consistency regularization
Key Contribution: Frequency perturbation via wavelet enhances boundaries in semi-supervised setting
Limitations / Gap: Semi-supervised only; wavelet for perturbation not feature decomposition
Relevance to Our Work: Shows wavelet helps boundaries even in semi-supervised; validates our frequency hypothesis

Paper Title: BGGL-Net: Boundary-Guided Global-Local Feature Fusion for Polyp Segmentation
Core Method: Local Info Encoder + Laplacian boundary extraction + cross-attention BGM
Key Contribution: Boundary features guide global feature fusion via cross-attention
Limitations / Gap: Laplacian-based boundary only; no frequency domain decomposition
Relevance to Our Work: Boundary-guided fusion for polyps — similar goal, different mechanism (spatial vs. our frequency)
